steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the land is "with some exaggeration called a garden," meaning that label is a stretch. The flower beds "confirmed" that illusion — they supplied visible evidence that made the exaggerated label feel real. The replacement word must mean "provided supporting evidence for."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Necessitated": Means "made necessary." Flower beds do not make the garden label necessary; they support it. ✗
  • "Substantiated": Means "gave substance or proof to." The blooming pansies substantiated the garden illusion by providing tangible, visible evidence. ✓
  • "Designated": Means "formally named or assigned." The flower beds do not officially name anything. ✗
  • "Promised": Means "gave reason to expect a future outcome." The confirmation here is present, not forward-looking. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
